[MI-048-009]Of course the little stream which fed the reservoir of Granite House and worked [[granite-house-lift|the lift]], had been carefully preserved, and the water could not fail. The lift once raised, this sure and comfortable retreat would be safe from any surprise.

The loch fills the lowest bed of the vast mine, and a sudden inrush of water served only to "raise the level of Loch Malcolm a few feet." (UC-016-011) Long after the danger has passed, the mine's tame harfang still haunts the water: "at long intervals, Nell would see the creature hovering above Loch Malcolm." (UC-019-008)

Manaos is the Brazilian city at the junction of the Amazon and the Rio Negro, one of the great landmarks of the raft's descent in *Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon*. It marks a change even in the river's name, for "from the frontier of Brazil to Manaos, where the superb Rio Negro joins it, it takes the name of the Solimaës." (EHLA-005-023)
